movies.title: Misan Harriman: Shoot The People
movies.cast: Misan Harriman
movies.description:
Shoot The People is a documentary capturing photographer and activist Misan Harriman’s journey documenting global protest movements that drive social change. Renowned for his wide-ranging work — including powerful breakthrough images from the 2020 Black Lives Matter protests and capturing the Palestinian liberation movement on film — Harriman also made history as the first Black man to photograph a British Vogue cover.

The film follows Harriman as he highlights the resilience of grassroots activists fighting for equality, civil rights, and social justice, the film showcases the intersectionality of these movements and their collective power. With historical context, interviews with activists, and explorations of digital activism, the documentary reveals how Harriman's lens brings the world's activism to light, inspiring viewers to recognize their own power in shaping a more just society.

movies.title: Mishima: A Life in Four Chapters
movies.description: Paul Schrader's visually stunning, collage-like portrait of acclaimed Japanese author and playwright Yukio Mishima (played by Ken Ogata) investigates the inner turmoil and contradictions of a man who attempted an impossible harmony between self, art, and society. Taking place on Mishima's last day, when he famously committed public seppuku, the film is punctuated by extended flashbacks to the writer's life, as well as by gloriously stylized evocations of his fictional works. With its rich cinematography by John Bailey, exquisite sets and costumes by Eiko Ishioka, and unforgettable, highly influential score by Philip Glass, MISHIMA: A LIFE IN FOUR CHAPTERS is a tribute to its subject and a bold, investigative work of art in its own right. (Janus)

movies.title: Mix Nyc Presents Barbara Hammer Through The Eyes Of Others
movies.description: Curated by Jac Renée Bruneau The pioneering lesbian experimental filmmaker Barbara Hammer produced more than 80 films between 1968 and 2019; this program showcases an array of works that explore her artistic output, her as a being, and the ripple effects both continue to have. The filmmaker began this process herself while she was alive— Audience offers a playful array of snippets from conversations about her work with attendees of numerous screenings. The program goes on to move through films by filmmakers who were/are in conversation with Hammer—from engaging in her practices to making homages, writing letters, and (upon Hammer’s invitation) even using her footage. Audience (1982) Dir. Barbara Hammer 32min Love, Barbara (2022) Dir. Brydie O’Connor 15min Untitled (Dyketactics Revisited) (2005) Dir. Liz Rosenfeld 13min Carolee, Barbara and Guvnor (2018) Dir. Lynne Sachs 9min A Video Letter to Barbara Hammer (2018) Dir. Joey Carducci 17min A Month of Single Frames (2019) Dir. Lynne Sachs 14min Followed by a Q&A with Brydie O’Connor and Joey Carducci, moderated by Jac Renée Bruneau

movies.title: Mubi Notebook Presents: I Am Love
movies.description: The new issue of Notebook , MUBI’s film journal, is devoted to the art of food on film. To celebrate, BAM is thrilled to screen Luca Guadagnino’s lush melodrama I Am Love , featuring an introduction by bestselling cookbook author Alison Roman and critic Elissa Suh. In turn-of-the-millennium Milan, the wife of a wealthy industrialist (Tilda Swinton) slips into a torrid affair with a talented chef (Edoardo Gabbriellini). For these lovers, food and sensuality are one and the same. From cakes to ukha, every dish he prepares only turns up the heat between them—and an unforgettable dish of glazed prawns is their gateway to unbridled passion.

movies.description: After dropping out of college and discovering the boy she likes doesn’t like her back, suburban Chen takes a job at a Taipei movie theater, where she meets Ling working in the ticket booth. Soon, a slow-simmering friendship grows between them in this sincere and irresistible Taiwanese coming-of-age film directed by Lin Cheng-sheng.

movies.title: Mysterious Object At Noon
movies.description: Apichatpong Weerasethakul brought an appetite for experimen­tation to Thai cinema with his debut feature, an uncategorizable work that refracts documentary impressions of his homeland through the surrealist concept of the “exquisite corpse.” Enlisting locals to contribute improvised narration to a simple tale, Weerasethakul charts the collective construction of the fiction as each new encounter imbues it with unpredictable shades of fantasy and pathos. Shot over the course of two years in black-and-white 16mm, Mysterious Object at Noon established the director’s defining fascination with the porous boundaries between the real and the imagined.

movies.title: Nadja
movies.description: Michael Almereyda’s ( Tesla , Experimenter , Hamlet ) 1994 work of horror—executive produced by David Lynch—centers on young vampire Nadja (Elina Löwensohn) as she attempts to reunite with her twin brother following their father’s death from a stake in the heart at the hands of Dr. Van Helsing (Peter Fonda). The cult-classic, which fuses shimmering black-and-white 35mm with the hallucinatory grain of Pixelvision video, draws inspiration from Dracula’s Daughter (1936) and André Breton’s surrealist novel Nadja . We’re thrilled to screen a brand-new 4K restoration of the director’s cut. A Grasshopper Film and Arbelos Films Release Featuring two Q&As with director Michael Almereyda, moderated on February 6 by critic Stephanie Zacharek at 7pm and on February 7 by filmmaker Azazel Jacobs at 7pm

movies.director: Nicolás Guillén Landrián This program of five short films offers an introduction to the subversive Cuban experimental filmmaker and painter Nicolás Guillén Landrián. En un bario Viejo
movies.description: This program of five short films offers an introduction to the subversive Cuban experimental filmmaker and painter Nicolás Guillén Landrián. En un bario Viejo (1963) 9min, digital Life in one of Havana's old neighborhoods is captured on film Los del baile (1965) 6min, digital People dance to a performance by Pello el Afrokán’s Orchestra in this short video essay. Like P.M. , the government censored Los del baile for presenting an “undesirable” image of Havana nightlife. Ociel del Toa (1965) 17min, digital A poetic rendering of the lives of the people who inhabit the banks of the Toa River in Cuba’s Oriente province. Reportaje (1966) 19min, SD file A group of the Cuban poor convene to discuss politics and eventually set a large rag-doll ablaze in an act of collective exorcism. Coffea Arábiga (1968) 18min, digital Commissioned by the government to produce a propaganda film on Cuban coffee, Landrián smuggled dissent into the project, turning it into a cinematic Trojan horse which drew a ban after the so-called Cuban “coffee plan” fell apart. Taller de Linea y 18 (1971) 15min, SD file Landrián’s first project to feature conventional documentary-style narration offers audiences a glimpse inside the workings of a bustling Havana factory.

Malmuth’s solo directorial debut, the project was plagued by production problems, with Stallone himself stepping in to help direct and re-edit aspects of the dark cat-and-mouse game. Despite being used as a shooting location for the 57th and 42nd Street stations within the film, a pesky Hoyt-Schermerhorn sign made its way into the film’s final cut.

movies.title: No Other Choice
movies.description: South Korea’s Academy Awards submission for Best Foreign Film is being hailed by critics as an utter masterpiece and earned a rapturous nine-minute standing ovation at this year’s Venice Film Festival. The visionary Park Chan-wook ( Oldboy , Decision to Leave ) helms this captivating black-comedy thriller—one of the most highly anticipated and acclaimed films of the season—adapted from Donald E. Westlake’s 1997 novel The Ax .

movies.title: Normal
movies.description:
The latest collaboration between Bob Odenkirk, JOHN WICK creator Derek Kolstad, and NOBODY producer Marc Provissiero, director Ben Wheatley’s (FREE FIRE, HIGH RISE) kinetic neo-Western stars Odenkirk as an unassuming substitute sheriff with a troubled past who, after moving to a small, sleepy town, responds to a bank robbery and unknowingly uncovers something far more explosive.
For Sheriff Ulysses (Odenkirk), his provisional posting to the quaint Midwestern American town of Normal was meant to be a welcome respite from both his marital woes and recent moral injuries in the line of duty. But when a botched bank robbery interrupts the municipality’s tranquil pace, a dark secret is inadvertently exposed, and Ulysses soon discovers that the town is anything but its namesake.

movies.description: Arrested after the killing of an Israeli soldier and tricked into an admission of guilt by association, a young Palestinian baker who routinely climbs over the separation wall running through Jerusalem to meet up with his girlfriend agrees to work as an informant. Nominated for the Best Foreign Feature Academy Award in 2014, Hany Abu-Assad’s film is both a thriller and a romance set against the tension of the Israeli occupation. Presented with the support of Friendly Strangers Studios. @friendlystrangersstudios on instagram.

movies.title: On Golden Pond
movies.description: Art can hold a mirror to reality, and provide a venue to catharsis in its retelling. The 1981 family drama On Golden Pond chronicles the relationship between curmudgeon Norman (Henry Fonda, in his final film appearance) and his estranged daughter Chelsea (Jane Fonda). When Norman and his wife Ethel (Hepburn) agree to care for Billy—the son of Chelsea’s new boyfriend—at their summer home, a blossoming new relationship provides an opportunity to heal old wounds. The latter Fonda had purchased the rights to the play on which the film was based specifically in hopes her father would portray the patriarch, and to provide an opportunity to recount a father-daughter rift that paralleled their real-life relationship.

movies.title: Over Your Dead Body (2026)
movies.description: When miserable couple Dan and Lisa retreat to a remote cabin for a romantic reset, each arrives with a secret plan to murder the other. Their carefully plotted traps and counterplots quickly unravel when a dangerous crew crashes the weekend with plans of their own. As the toxic getaway spirals into chaotic carnage, Dan and Lisa must survive the intruders, each other, and figure out if they want to save their marriage or survive it.

movies.title: Palestine ‘36
movies.description: In 1936, Yusuf (Karim Daoud Anaya) oscillates between his rural home in Mandatory Palestine and an increasingly restless Jerusalem. Chronicling what would become the largest uprising against Britain’s 30-year colonial project in the region, writer-director Annemarie Jacir (who was last at BAM for a full retrospective in 2018) returns with her most ambitious project to date. Anchored by performances from Hiam Abbass ( Succession ) and Saleh Bakri ( All That’s Left of You ), the only feature to shoot in Palestine in the last two years arrives at a critical time. Advance screening followed by a Q&A with Annemarie Jacir on Mar 20, followed by our run of the film starting Mar 27

movies.description: Timid Colin (Melling) is swept off his feet when Ray (Skarsgård), an enigmatic, impossibly handsome biker, takes him on as his submissive. Subversive, sexy, and surprisingly tender, this unconventional romance between an inquisitive young gay man and his leatherman lover was written by director Harry Lighton, based on the 2020 novel Box Hill by Adam Mars-Jones.

movies.title: Playtime
movies.description:
Jacques Tati’s gloriously choreographed, nearly wordless comedies about confusion in an age of high technology reached their apotheosis with PLAYTIME. For this monumental achievement, a nearly three-year-long bank-breaking production, Tati again thrust the lovably old-fashioned Monsieur Hulot, along with a host of other lost souls, into a baffling modern world, this time, Paris. With every inch of its super-wide frame crammed with hilarity and inventiveness, PLAYTIME is a lasting record of a modern era tiptoeing on the edge of oblivion.
DCP courtesy of Janus Films

movies.title: Power to the People: John & Yoko Live in NYC
movies.description:
POWER TO THE PEOPLE: JOHN & YOKO LIVE IN NYC is the 2026 multiscreen concert film of two massive live shows by John Lennon & Yoko Ono at Madison Square Garden, New York City, on August 30, 1972, newly restored, re-edited, and remixed by the Lennons’ seven-times GRAMMY®-Award winning team.
John & Yoko/Plastic Ono Band with Elephant's Memory and Special Guests performed these now-legendary sold-out One To One concerts to a combined audience of 40,000 people, raising over $1.5M (equivalent to $11.5M in 2026) for children with intellectual and developmental disabilities. They were the only full-length concerts John Lennon (with Yoko Ono) performed after leaving The Beatles.
Originally filmed by multi-camera director Steve Gebhardt in 1972, this 2026 version of the concert film is directed by Simon Hilton, edited by Ben Wainwright-Pearce, and produced by Peter Worsley & Sean Ono Lennon. Music Production is by Sean Ono Lennon; mixed and engineered by Paul Hicks & Sam Gannon.
Hits performed include John's "New York City," "Instant Karma!," "Imagine," and "Mother," plus Yoko's "Don't Worry Kyoko" and "Open Your Box," plus rousing renditions of "Come Together" and "Hound Dog," and an encore of "Give Peace a Chance" with special guests Stevie Wonder, Melanie, and many others.

movies.title: Project Hail Mary
movies.cast: Ryan Gosling, Milana Vayntrub, Ken Leung, Sandra Hüller
movies.description: School-teacher-turned-astronaut Ryland Grace (Gosling) awakes on a space station with no memory of who he is or his mission. As fragments of memory return in bursts, he pieces together his objective to reverse the impact of a space event that hurled Earth into the early stages of an Ice Age, and soon realizes he may not be alone in his quest. Based on the novel by Andy Weir ( The Martian ), Phil Lord and Christopher Miller’s adaptation invokes elements of puppetry and practical effects in its conjuring of Grace’s companion, propelling a visual style reminiscent of some of sci-fi’s trailblazing works.

movies.title: Project Hail Mary
movies.cast: Ryan Gosling, Milana Vayntrub, Ken Leung, Sandra Hüller
movies.description: Based on Andy Weirʼs (The Martian) novel of the same name, PROJECT HAIL MARY is an exhilarating space adventure set in the near future. Ryland Grace (Ryan Gosling), a school-teacher-turned-astronaut, wakes up from a coma, alone, on a space station with no memory of who he is or his mission. His memory returns in bursts, and he pieces together that he was sent to the Tau Ceti solar system, 12 light-years from Earth, to reverse the impact of a space event that had already hurled our planet into the early stages of an Ice Age. As details of the mission unravel, Grace must call on all of his scientific training and sheer ingenuity, but he might not have to do it alone…

movies.title: Reel Sisters & Bam Present An Evening Of Shorts Honoring Women’S History Month
movies.description: Join BAM and Reel Sisters for an evening of family-centered films in honor of Women’s History Month on March 25, 2026 from 7 pm-9 pm. Enjoy a powerful selection of notable award-winning films from Reel Sisters of the Diaspora Film Festival & Lecture Series, including our 2025 Oscar qualifying short Shallow End and the highly acclaimed Superman Doesn’t Steal directed by Tamika Lamison. Founded in 1997 by African Voices magazine, Reel Sisters, the first Oscar-qualifying film festival in the nation devoted to women of color, has showcased 10,000+ films by women of African, Asian, Native American, Indian, Latina, and Arabic descent. Reel Sisters will be held in Brooklyn on Oct. 24-25, 2026. For information visit www.reelsisters.org . Film Showcase 2026 Musica Quarantena (2022) Dir. Lilian T. Mehrel Producers: Lilian T. Mehrel, Danielle Rhoda Animator: Danielle Rhoda Animated, 4min A little girl’s love for her bedridden Papa inspires a town in lockdown to make music out of their windows in this animated short starring Elisa del Genio (HBO’s My Brilliant Friend ). Gloria (2025) Dir. Kim Blanck Producers: Xin Liu, Caitlyn Durkin, Kim Blanck, Caitlin Machak Narrative, 10min Gloria, a Chinese woman in her 70s living alone and struggling with her memory, finds new purpose in teaching herself Spanish. Reel Sisters Best Narrative Short 2025 (Academy Qualifying Film) Shallow End (2025) Dir. Maria de la Cruz Rudloff Producer: Maria de la Cruz Rudloff, Janet Valentina Manina, Anne Leigh Cooper Narrative, 11min. Twenty-something year old Agatha comes home a shattered person after a lengthy stay in rehab. In her small town, everything seems to remind her of the life she once had and the people she held dear before it all went wrong. Laundry (2025) Dir. Okema T. Moore Producers: Alterik Miller, Danielle Moné Truitt, Samuel Calvin Morrison, Jr., Tobias Truvillion, Wink Woodall Writer: Alterik Miller Narrative, 19min. Laundry follows Darius and Bree Williams, a couple whose lives are shattered when their six-year-old son Hunter suffers a severe oxygen-depriving accident. The short stars Tobias Truvillion ( Empire , First Wives Club ) and Danielle Moné Truitt ( Law & Order: Organized Crime ). Reel Sisters Best Short Comedy 2025 The Knife (2024) Dir. Joy Nesbitt Producer: Grace Odumosu Narrative, 15 min. Ife, a young Black Irish woman, is anxiously awaiting her 20-week pregnancy scan, hoping for good news about her baby’s health. When the scan reveals unexpected complications, she and her husband, Darragh, are forced to confront a ridiculous reality that will test their trust in the healthcare system. Reel Sisters Best Director 2024 Superman Doesn’t Steal (2024) Dir. Tamika Lamison Producers: Tamika Lamison, Chris Beal, Dominique DeLeon, Carol Shine, Ben Epraim Narrative, 19 min. Based on true events, Superman Doesn't Steal is a coming-of-age story set during the 1970s Atlanta child murders—seen through the eyes of nine-year-old Harriet and her brother, two children fascinated with superheroes.

movies.description: “What kind of Jew are you?” “A German Jew! The kind who goes to synagogue on Yom Kippur and sings ‘Silent Night’ on Christmas!”

movies.title: Rituals For The Dead And Living
movies.description: Cyclical magic and mythic imaginaries link the films of four interdisciplinary artists who document the movements of individual and collective bodies. our songs were ready for all wars to come (2021) Dir. Noor Abed 20 min, digital In Arabic with English subtitles Noor Abed’s haunted choreography translates Palestinian folk tales into grainy Super 8, accompanied by the voice of singer Maya Khald. The short film meditates on communal rituals associated with disappearance, mourning, and death, rewriting the history and present of a people and their land while reflecting on the film itself as a form of mediation. Ritual in Transfigured Time (1946) Dir. Maya Deren 15 min, digital In Maya Deren’s film, the ritual of a look and the ritual of social gathering weave together in a symbolic world of gesture. Solemnly silent, Ritual in Transfigured Time is a psychodrama of bodies in motion that reflects Deren’s intensely choreographic approach to cinema. Inauguration of the Pleasure Dome (1954) Dir. Kenneth Anger 38 min, digital Decadent, provocative, and surreal, this work fuses Soviet montage, baroque opulence, and non-narrative symbolism to present a fever-dream masquerade of revelers staging occult ceremonies of magic and ecstatic eroticism. Without Your Interpretation (1983) Dir. Ulysses Jenkins 14 min, digital In the early 1980s, Ulysses Jenkins documented a twilight dance in downtown Los Angeles. Playing with the then-emergent music video format, he intercut choreography by the Rudy Perez troupe—as well as the artists Senga Nengudi and Maren Hassinger—with found footage of musical performances and news media, orchestrating a sharp critique of American culture and militarism.

movies.title: Riverbend
movies.description: Note: This screening is postponed as guests are unable to travel due to weather. A new date will be announced soon. All ticketholders will be refunded. Reelblack Renaissance re-releases Sam Firstenberg’s ( American Ninja ) action-drama Riverbend in a brand new 6K restoration. Fugitives from an unjust military court-martial, Black Vietnam soldiers Samuel Quinton (James), Tony Marx (Tennon), and Butch Turner (Morris) seek refuge in the remote town of Riverbend, Georgia. When they discover that Riverbend’s Black residents live under the thumb of an oppressive sheriff (Frank), the soldiers join forces with the townspeople to fight back. Originally released on VHS in 1989 and long obscured from view, the film has since been rediscovered as a vital story of Black resistance. We’re thrilled to screen this long-lost, action-packed classic for the world premiere of the new restoration! Featuring a Q&A with director Sam Firstenberg and actors Julius Tennon and Vanessa Tate, moderated by Melissa Lyde of Alfreda’s Cinema

movies.title: Robert Wilson And The Civil Wars
movies.description: One of the great unrealized theatrical productions of the 20th century, the CIVIL warS: a tree is best measured when it is down would have been visionary director Robert Wilson’s magnum opus: a 12-hour historical opera in six distinct parts, each prepared in a different country ahead of a complete performance planned for the 1984 Summer Olympics in Los Angeles. Director Howard Brookner’s 1985 documentary follows Wilson as he collaborates with artists like David Byrne, Philip Glass, and Lucinda Childs, while grappling with funding challenges, labor issues, and the daunting scale of his undertaking. After elements of the film were lost or destroyed in Superstorm Sandy, Aaron Brookner, the documentarian’s nephew, devoted 12 years to restoring this extraordinary chronicle. Restored in 2025 by Pinball London – Janus Films – The Criterion Collection at Pinball London, Bando a parte, Dan Zlotnik, and Matar Studio from a 16mm print, a VHS, and the stereo 16mm optical sound. Funding provided by Howard Brookner Legacy Project, Howard Brookner Estate, Pinball London and Janus Films – The Criterion Collection. Restoration supervised by Aaron Brookner, Paula Vaccaro, and Carlos Morales/EPost.

movies.title: Rocco And His Brothers
movies.cast:
Alain Delon, 
Renato Salvatori, 
Annie Girardot, 
Katina Paxinou, 
Roger Hanin, 
Paolo Stoppa, 
Suzy Delair, 
Claudia Cardinale
movies.description: A grieving woman testing her limits in the Australian wilderness is suddenly ensnared in a deadly game with a ruthless predator.

movies.title: RZA's One Spoon of Chocolate
movies.description: RZA’S ONE SPOON OF CHOCOLATE follows Unique (Shameik Moore, DOPE and voice of Miles Morales in the Spider-Verse films), a veteran and ex-convict seeking a fresh start in a small town. After an altercation with a gang of locals, he starts to suspect they may have something to do with the disappearance of young men in the area – including his cousin. As he digs for the truth, he finds himself the target of not only the gang but the local sheriff’s office, whose involvement in the disappearances may be even more sinister. Instead of waiting for his turn to be picked off, Unique and those closest to him (Paris Jackson, RJ Cyler) decide to fight back in this sharply satirical and stylish action thriller from the visionary leader of the Wu-Tang Clan.

movies.title: Sambizanga
movies.description: Sarah Maldoror, the first woman to make a feature film in Africa, chronicles Angola’s anti-colonialist struggle via the story of a woman’s relentless search for her resistance leader husband after he’s arrested and brutally imprisoned by the Portuguese authorities. Infused with the spirits of Marx and Frantz Fanon, Sambizanga is a love story, a bracing dramatization of a pivotal moment in Angola’s fight for freedom and a rousing call to revolution.

movies.title: Sara Gómez – Islands
movies.director: Sara Gómez Gómez explores life outside the capital city through her short films, the “Island Trilogy,” centered on the storied Isla de Pinos, and a magnetic portrait of the port city of Santiago de Cuba. En la otra isla
movies.description: Gómez explores life outside the capital city through her short films, the “Island Trilogy,” centered on the storied Isla de Pinos, and a magnetic portrait of the port city of Santiago de Cuba. En la otra isla (1968) 41min, DCP Assembling honest, varied and insightful testimonials and sometimes appearing on screen herself, Sara Gómez interviewed the residents of the Isla de Pinos. Renamed the Isla de la Juventud (“Island of Youth”), it served the collective education of young people from Havana, assigned to collective farms. Speaking to an aspiring hairdresser, an ex-priest and former opera singer, the documentary offers a varied perspective of their experiences, opinions, and dreams in the first decade of the revolution. Una isla para Miguel (1968) 22min, DCP The second installment of the “Island Trilogy” is centered on the rebellious Miguel, a student and worker at one of the state’s country-side schools. It reveals a cross-section of issues of individual behavior and collective discipline, the construction of masculinity and marginalized populations. Isla del Tesoro (1969) 9min, DCP Another view of the island, which was the site of Castro’s imprisonment from 1953 to 1955, explores its carceral history and post-revolutionary repurposing by exploring the past of Presidio Modelo prison and present of citrus production. Iré a Santiago (1964) 15min, DCP In one of her earliest works, Gómez made the camera dance through a charming city portrait of Santiago de Cuba. Her exploration of the port city acts as an ironic travelogue and historical retelling which foregrounds the musical culture of Afro-Cubans. Her vibrant short film interweaves the colonial past of the wider Black Caribbean, including Haiti, with a documentation of the possibilities of the revolutionary present, unfolding in nocturnal revelry of a street celebration.

movies.title: Saturday Afternoon Cartoons: Sweet Treats
movies.description: Introduction and Q&A by curator Tommy Stathes on Saturday, April 18th

movies.title: School Daze With Spike Lee
movies.description: Spike Lee’s audacious follow-up to She’s Gotta Have It (originally released on February 12, 1988) follows A Self-Styled radical student (Laurence Fishburne) at The Historically Black Mission College as he attempts to shake up The Campus’ Political Conscience and clashes with The Macho “Dog Or Die” Gamma Phi Gamma Fraternity. Bursting with Energetic Musical Numbers (including “Good and Bad Hair” and The Number 1 Hit “Da Butt”) School Daze pulls off a deft mix of Comedy and Commentary as it explores Colorism, Class and African American Identity. Spike Lee Will Introduce School Daze at both screenings.

movies.title: Screwdriver
movies.description: Ziad, a young Palestinian basketball hero, has been imprisoned after assassinating an Israeli settler who killed his best friend. Shot entirely on location in the West Bank with a largely Palestinian crew, director Bassam Jarbawi’s debut feature follows Ziad as he returns home 15 years later, emerging into a world he can scarcely recognize and haunted by memories and hallucinations.

movies.title: Sentimental Value
movies.description: Sisters Nora (Reinsve) and Agnes (Lilleaas) reunite with their estranged father, the charismatic Gustav (Skarsgård, in a remarkable performance), a once-renowned director who offers stage actress Nora a role in what he hopes will be his comeback film. When she declines, Gustav casts an up-and-coming young Hollywood star (Fanning) instead, forcing the family to confront long-buried tensions as the striving outsider becomes entangled in their fraught dynamic. With Sentimental Value , Danish-Norwegian auteur Joachim Trier follows his critically acclaimed The Worst Person in the World (2021) with a magnetic comedy-drama about legacy, art, and the performances that bind—and divide—families.

movies.title: Set It Off
movies.description: Four struggling women stick it to society by pulling a string of bank robberies in this female-powered heist thriller. The action sequences pack an impressive visceral jolt, but director Gray ( Friday ) adds a gritty layer of social realism by delving into the circumstances that drive each woman to a life of crime. “Queen Latifah gives a career-making performance” ( Variety ) as a brash carjacker.

movies.title: Sherlock Jr. + One Week
movies.description: One Week (1920) Dirs. Buster Keaton & Edward F. Cline With Keaton, Joe Roberts, Sybil Seely 19min; DCP Keaton’s first film production released on his own, One Week marked a watershed moment in the revolutionary actor’s development of his distinct physical comedy and meticulously executed stunts. The story, a simple tale of Buster attempting to build a house for his new bride, is rife with Keaton’s now-iconic ladder gags, and originated the oft-reused gag of him emerging unscathed in the aftermath of a building collapse. Sherlock, Jr. (1924) Dir. Buster Keaton With Keaton, Kathryn McGuire, Ward Crane, Joe Keaton 45min; DCP Longer and more narratively intricate than One Week , Keaton’s 1924 tale of a projectionist daydreaming himself directly into the movies (especially as Conan Doyle’s master detective) may have not delivered the same level of kneeslapping as his previous work, but instead established him as a more philosophically-complex auteur. Restored by Cineteca di Bologna at L'Immagine Ritrovata Laboratory in association with Cohen Film Collection.

movies.title: Show Me Love
movies.description: Set in the small Swedish town of Åmål, this cherished coming‑of‑age film follows Agnes (Rebecka Liljeberg), a creative and lonely teenager who has long been secretly in love with Elin (Alexandra Dahlström), the cute, rebellious sister of the school’s most popular girl. As the only visibly queer student, Agnes endures bothersome bullying but finds refuge in poetry and the moody alternative music she treasures. Around her, classmates try to play into a world shaped by contemporary pop culture and the teen‑idol wave of the late ’90s (including Robyn, an iconic pride of Sweden). When Agnes and Elin’s paths—and angsty vibes—finally intersect, Agnes’s long‑held crush proves a challenge to the town’s conservative notions, revealing a tenderly defiant story of budding desire, courage, and self‑acceptance.

movies.title: Silent Friend
movies.cast: Tony Leung Chiu-wai, Lea Seydoux, Luna Wedler, Corrine Le Hong
movies.description: Set in the botancial garden of a medieval town in Germany. Three epochs, three personalities, three sometimes clumsy but sincere attempts to free themselves and to create links with the plants and the works of the garden.

movies.title: Simone Barbès Or Virtue
movies.description: Set over the course of a single night, Simone Barbès or Virtue follows a young porn theater employee who drifts through Paris, finding chance encounters, sexual transactions, and fleeting conversations that blur the line between desire and morality. Marie-Claude Treilhou’s stylish feature debut from 1980 is a brilliant work of New Wave inspired cinema. Featuring a reading by Katie Lane before the screening on February 15.

movies.title: Sirât
movies.description: Searching for his missing daughter, middle-aged Luis (Sergi López) travels with his son Esteban (Bruno Núñez Arjona) to a rave in the mountains of Morocco. Bound for a mythic party deep in the desert as an armed conflict erupts with a neighboring nation, Luis, Esteban, and a small band of ravers confront perilous conditions—a journey evoking the Arabic word sirât , in Islam a reference to the razor-thin bridge all must cross on the Day of Judgment, when only the righteous survive. Awash in penetrating light and pulsating sound, Sirāt shared the Jury Prize at this year’s Cannes Film Festival. 2026 Academy Award nominee, Best International Feature Film, Best Sound

movies.title: Ski Party
movies.description: In this musical, college classmates Todd Armstrong (Frankie Avalon) and Craig Gamble (Dwayne Hickman) head out on a group ski vacation, and agree that if they're going to get girlfriends, they need to understand how women think.

movies.title: Soleil Ô
movies.description: Introduction by Yassine Ait Ali, PhD candidate at Princeton University and founding director of the Princeton French Film Festival on Sunday, April 26th

movies.title: Solomon Northup’S Odyssey
movies.description: New 4K Restoration by IndieCollect Directed by the legendary Gordon Parks and based on Northup's 1853 memoir, this tour de force recounts the true story of a free Black man kidnapped in 1841 and sold into slavery. The film predates 12 Years a Slave by 30 years and equals its narrative power. Originally broadcast on public television as part of the "American Playhouse" series, Solomon Northup's Odyssey was never seen in theaters—until now. For this new 4K restoration, IndieCollect scanned the original camera negative and did extensive color correction in consultation with DP Hiro Narita. The restoration was made possible with support from Ford Foundation/JustFilms and Golden Globe Foundation. Q&A on Friday, 4/3 at 7pm with Eva Yuma, restoration colorist, and Melissa Howell, 3rd great-granddaughter of Solomon Northup, moderated by Sandra Schulberg, founder of Indiecollect Q&A following the 4/4, 5:30pm screening with Dr. Sheril Antonio (NYU professor and author of Contemporary African American Cinema ) in conversation with Warrington Hudlin, co-founder of Black Filmmaker Foundation

movies.title: Song Sung Blue
movies.description: Written and directed by Craig Brewer ( Hustle & Flow , Dolemite Is My Name ), Song Sung Blue is based on a 2009 documentary of the same name. Recounting the true story of Mike and Claire Sardina (Jackman and Hudson), two down-on-their-luck musicians who form a joyous Neil Diamond tribute band, Lightning & Thunder, Song Sung Blue suggests it's never too late to find love and follow your dreams—and that singing a pop hit like its originator can allow an earnest belter to tap into something magical. “Brewer navigates this terrain like a jukebox Jonathan Demme,” Variety proclaims.

movies.title: Southern Comfort
movies.description: 25th Anniversary screening This vibrant vérité documentary, winner of the Grand Jury Prize at 2001’s Sundance Film Festival, traces the final year in the life of Robert Eads, a transgender man and community leader living in rural Georgia. Southern Comfort moves with quiet intimacy and wit, following Eads as he opens his world with disarming honesty: his romantic partnership with Lola, his loving yet complicated bond with his son, and the chosen family that surrounds him across the rural South. As Eads candidly confronts being denied lifesaving treatment by a failing healthcare system, the film transforms that injustice into a profoundly human portrait. Director Kate Davis captures a community navigating love, caretaking, and identity in a region that often refuses to see them. Eads’ appearance at the Southern Comfort Conference in Atlanta, an important gathering for trans people, becomes both elegy and testament: a reminder of the resilience and interconnectedness that sustain trans lives throughout America.

movies.title: Stalker
movies.description: Tarkovsky’s stunning, haunted sepia-toned sci-fi masterpiece follows a scientist and a writer who, living in a broken-down totalitarian dystopia, recruit the help of a “Stalker”—a kind of post-apocalyptic Sherpa—to guide them on a voyage of…

movies.title: Strange Journey: The Story of Rocky Horror
movies.description: A London theater play evolves into a groundbreaking cult phenomenon, featuring iconic songs and performances that celebrate individuality. The legacy lives on through midnight screenings and a devoted following that spans generations.

movies.title: Suspiria
movies.description: Argento’s supernatural horror classic stars Jessica Harper ( Phantom of the Paradise , Pennies from Heaven ) as a young student who discovers a demonic underworld haunting the halls of a dance academy in West Germany. The first film in Argento’s The Three Mothers trilogy, this celluloid nightmare moves between elements of sheer horror and flashes of pure sensory delirium, set to a pounding prog-rock dirge by Goblin. Fifty years on, Suspiria feels as dangerous and decadent as ever.

movies.title: Suzhou River
movies.description: Taking its name from the polluted river that flows through Shanghai, director Lou’s hometown, the brooding Suzhou River uses the singular first-person perspective of its unseen videographer-narrator to explore the grubby underbelly of the city,…

movies.title: Tahar Cheriaa: A Shorts Program
movies.description: Q&A with filmmaker and curator Mohamed Challouf moderated by Jordan Coty Eloundou Ndongo, Princeton University PhD Student, on Saturday, April 18th

movies.title: Tahar Cheriaa: Under the Shadow of the Baobab
movies.description: Q&A with Director and Film Curator Mohamed Challouf moderated by Alia Ayman, writer curator and co-founder of Zawya on Friday, April 17th

movies.title: Taking Of Pelham 123
movies.description: The third, and most recent, adaptation of John Godey’s 1973 novel, Taking of Pelham 123 charts one of the most iconic subway stories ever put to film. When ‘Ryder’ (Travolta) and his accomplices hijack a 6 train, dispatcher Walter Garber (Washington) is thrust into the position of negotiator. Despite the 6 train setting, most of the film was shot on the track between Hoyt-Schermerhorn and the Transit Museum in Downtown Brooklyn. With a roster of iconic New York/Jersey actors, Scott’s adaptation—arriving near the end of his visually and editorially baroque late period—thrusts a timeless story into the modern era.

movies.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
movies.cast: Brian Tochi, Josh Pais, Corey Feldman, Robbie Rist
movies.description: The first film adaptation of Kevin Eastman and Peter Laird’s four anthropomorphic comic book characters, TMNT sends the lovable heroes on a quest to save Splinter. With turtle costumes by Jim Henson’s Creature Shop, the 1990 adaptation employs a combination of puppetry and set design to convey the cartoonish NYC of its ninjutsu-trained brothers. When the Turtles’ reporter pal April exits Manhattan’s City Hall, a keen viewer could note that she actually descends into the Hoyt-Schermerhorn station.

movies.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
movies.cast: Brian Tochi, Josh Pais, Corey Feldman, Robbie Rist

With turtle costumes by Jim Henson’s Creature Shop, the 1990 adaptation employs a combination of puppetry and set design to convey the cartoonish NYC of its ninjutsu-trained brothers. When the Turtles’ reporter pal April exits Manhattan’s City Hall, a keen viewer could note that she actually descends into the Hoyt-Schermerhorn station.

movies.title: Terror of Mechagodzilla
movies.description:
Evil jumpsuit-wearing spacemen from the Black Hole are plotting to flatten Tokyo and build their own utopian city. Aided by deranged scientist Dr. Mafune, shunned for his strange experiments on sea life, the aliens unleash a rebuilt Mechagodzilla (this IS a direct sequel to the first Mechagodzilla jam) and the monster Titanosaurus (which looks like an aquatic Godzilla whose special attack is causing windstorms with his fins) to do their evil bidding.
What better revenge is there for a dismissed scientist than to take control of giant monsters and use them for ultimate destruction? Eventually, Godzilla surfaces to defend his homeland, and that’s when the Earth-shattering destruction begins.
Note #1: This is the only Godzilla movie where he actually catches on fire.
Note #2: Mechagodzilla can fight without his head! What more could you ask for?

movies.title: That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime the Movie: Tears of the Azure Sea (Dubbed)
movies.description:
Presented in an English dub
After concluding the opening ceremony of the Demon Kingdom Federation Tempest, Rimuru and his companions are invited by the Celestial Emperor Hermesia of the great elven nation – the Magi Dynasty Salion – to visit her private resort island. As the group enjoys their brief vacation, a mysterious woman named Yura appears. A new incident unfolds against the backdrop of the boundless azure sea.

movies.description:

After concluding the opening ceremony of the Demon Kingdom Federation Tempest, Rimuru and his companions are invited by the Celestial Emperor Hermesia of the great elven nation – the Magi Dynasty Salion – to visit her private resort island. As the group enjoys their brief vacation, a mysterious woman named Yura appears. A new incident unfolds against the backdrop of the boundless azure sea.

movies.title: The Big Lebowski
movies.description: The Coen’s cult comedy par excellence is the rare film not to contain a single unmemorable character or performance, from John Goodman’s John Milius-inspired gun nut Walter Sobchak to John Turturro’s snake-hipped sex offender Jesus Quintana to, of…

movies.title: The Blue Caftan
movies.description: Halim and Mina run a traditional caftan store in one of Morocco’s oldest medinas, and hire Youssef in order to keep up with customer demand. Mina gradually realizes how much her husband is moved by the young man’s presence. Morocco’s official entry for Best International Film at the 95th Academy Awards, The Blue Caftan tells a surprising, tender story of unconditional love and acceptance.

movies.title: The China Syndrome
movies.description: Named for the fictional result of a nuclear meltdown (where reactor fuel melts through the crust of the Earth all the way “to China”), Bridges’ biting thriller traces a reporter’s investigation of a cover-up of safety hazards at a Los Angeles nuclear plant. A gripping and timely depiction of the decade’s nuclear suspicion, the film’s coincidental (and perhaps prescient) release twelve days prior to the Three Mile Island nuclear accident spawned unanticipated conversation and acclaim. Both Fonda and Lemmon received Oscar nods for their portrayals of two strangers bound by a mutually terrifying discovery.

movies.title: The Christophers
movies.cast: Michaela Coel, Ian McKellen, James Corden, Jessica Gunning
movies.description: Two siblings conspire to forge their father's unfinished paintings so they become valuable after his death

movies.title: The Christophers
movies.cast: Michaela Coel, Ian McKellen, James Corden, Jessica Gunning
movies.description: The estranged children (Corden, Gunning) of a beloved dormant artist (McKellen) hire Lori (Coel), an art restorer and former forger, to pose as a prospective assistant in an effort to access some unfinished canvases they believe to be instrumental to conjuring a worthwhile inheritance. As always, Steven Soderbergh is a master of embedding the formal and technical flourishes of the arthouse inside seemingly populist fare, as exemplified in his Ocean ’s trilogy and last year’s Black Bag (2025). In turns that Deadline deemed an "unusually emotional film for Soderbergh,” McKellen’s and Coel’s tête-à-tête makes for a quintessentially funny and engrossing addition to the director’s oeuvre.

movies.title: The Christophers
movies.cast: Michaela Coel, Ian McKellen, James Corden, Jessica Gunning
movies.description: Julian Sklar was once a star of London’s 1960s and '70s pop art explosion, but he hasn’t painted in decades and has been broke for years. His two estranged children, desperate for an inheritance, hire Lori, an art restorer and former forger, to pose as a prospective assistant in order to access eight unfinished canvases Julian has buried deep in storage. Her plan is to complete them, then return them to storage, where they are to be “discovered” upon Julian’s death.

movies.title: The Darjeeling Limited
movies.description: Still in mourning—some more obviously than others—a year after their father’s funeral, three estranged brothers (Owen Wilson, Adrien Brody, and Jason Schwartzman, also one of the film’s screenwriters) set off on a “spiritual journey” across…

movies.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2
movies.cast: Meryl Streep, Emily Blunt, Anne Hathaway, Stanley Tucci
movies.description:
THE DEVIL WEARS PRADA 2 contains several sequences with flashing lights that may affect those who are susceptible to photosensitive epilepsy or have other photosensitivities.
Twenty years after making their iconic turns as Miranda, Andy, Emily, and Nigel, Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt, and Stanley Tucci return to the fashionable streets of New York City and the sleek offices of Runway Magazine in the eagerly awaited sequel to the 2006 phenomenon that defined a generation.
